chapter_title.ar: باب صَدَقَاتِ النَّبِيِّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ فَاطِمَةَ وَ الْأَئِمَّةِ ( عليهم السلام ) وَ وَصَايَاهُمْ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 35 – The charities of the Prophet<sup>saww</sup>, and Fatima<sup>asws</sup>, and the Imams<sup>asws</sup>, and their<sup>asws</sup> bequests

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/82)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/82)
text: قَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) أَ لَا أُقْرِئُكَ وَصِيَّةَ فَاطِمَةَ ( عليها السلام ) قَالَ قُلْتُ بَلَى قَالَ فَأَخْرَجَ حُقّاً أَوْ سَفَطاً فَأَخْرَجَ مِنْهُ كِتَاباً فَقَرَأَهُ بِسْمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحْمَنِ الرَّحِيمِ هَذَا مَا أَوْصَتْ بِهِ فَاطِمَةُ بِنْتُ مُحَمَّدٍ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) أَوْصَتْ بِحَوَائِطِهَا السَّبْعَةِ الْعَوَافِ وَ الدَّلَالِ وَ الْبُرْقَةِ وَ الْمَيْثَبِ وَ الْحُسْنَى وَ الصَّافِيَةِ وَ مَا لِأُمِّ إِبْرَاهِيمَ إِلَى عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي طَالِبٍ ( عليه السلام ) فَإِنْ مَضَى عَلِيٌّ فَإِلَى الْحَسَنِ فَإِنْ مَضَى الْحَسَنُ فَإِلَى الْحُسَيْنِ فَإِنْ مَضَى الْحُسَيْنُ فَإِلَى الْأَكْبَرِ مِنْ وُلْدِي
شَهِدَ اللَّهُ عَلَى ذَلِكَ وَ الْمِقْدَادُ بْنُ الْأَسْوَدِ وَ الزُّبَيْرُ بْنُ الْعَوَّامِ وَ كَتَبَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ أَبِي طَالِبٍ .
وَ عَنْهُ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ عَاصِمِ بْنِ حُمَيْدٍ مِثْلَهُ وَ لَمْ يَذْكُرْ حُقّاً وَ لَا سَفَطاً وَ قَالَ إِلَى الْأَكْبَرِ مِنْ وُلْدِي دُونَ وُلْدِكَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Majran, from Aasim Bin Hameed, from Abu Baseer who said,

‘Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Shall I<sup>asws</sup> read out to you the bequest of Fatima<sup>asws</sup>?’ I said, ‘Yes’. So he<sup>asws</sup> brought out a box or a basket, and extracted a letter from it. So he<sup>asws</sup> read: ‘In the Name of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> the Beneficent, the Merciful. This is what has been bequeathed by Fatima<sup>asws</sup> daughter of Muhammad<sup>saww</sup>, Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>. I<sup>asws</sup> hereby bequeath these seven walls (walled gardens) – Al-Dallal, and Al-Barqa, and Al-Maysab, and Al-Husna, and Al-Safiya, and what was for mother of Ibrahim<sup>as</sup>, to Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Abu Talib<sup>asws</sup>. So if he<sup>asws</sup> was to pass away, so to Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup>. So if Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> was to pass away, so to Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup>. So if Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> was to pass away, so to the elder from my<sup>asws</sup> children’.
Allah<sup>azwj</sup> was a Witness upon that, and Al-Miqdad Bin Al-Aswad, and Al-Zubeyr Bin Al-Awwam; and it was written by Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Abu Talib<sup>asws</sup>’.

And from him, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Aasim Bin Hameed, similar to it, but did not mention a box or a basket, and she<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘To the eldest of my<sup>asws</sup> children besides your<sup>asws</sup> children’.
en.sarwar: 5. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu Najran from ‘Asem ibn Humayd from abu Basir who has narrated the following: “Once abu Ja’far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, decided to inform us about an important fact. He (the Imam) said, ‘Do you like if I read the will of Fatimah, ‘Alayha al-Salam, for you?’ I replied, ‘Yes, I like to hear.’ He (the Imam) then took out a box or a basket from which he took out a book (a letter) and read, ‘In the name of Allah, the Beneficent, the Merciful. This is the will of Fatimah, daughter of Muhammad, the Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause. She; bequests all of her seven walls (gardens enclosed by walls), al-‘Awaf, al- Dalal, al-Burqah, al-Maythab, al-Husna’, al-Safiyah and Ma li ’Umm Ibrahim to Ali ibn abu Talib, ‘Alayhi al-Salam. When Ali passes away it then is for al-Hassan, when al-Hassan passes away then it is for al-Husayn and when al-Husayn passes away then it is for the eldest of my children. Allah is witness thereof, al-Miqdad ibn Aswad, al-Zubayr ibn al-‘Awam and Ali ibn abu Talib are the scribes. From the narrator of the previous Hadith from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from ‘ Asem ibn Humayd a similar Hadith is narrated in which he has not mentioned box or basket and has said ... To the eldest of my children other than [not] your children.’
